articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Stricter EIA Norms, Prioritizes Environmental Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, upheld the validity of recent amendments to the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) notification, 2020, which introduced more stringent regulations.
• The ruling emphasizes the 'polluter pays' principle and the need for comprehensive pre-project environmental assessments.
• It rejected arguments for expedited clearances at the cost of ecological integrity, stressing the intergenerational equity aspect of environmental resources.
• The judgment also highlighted the importance of public consultation in the EIA process, making it a non-negotiable component for category A and B1 projects.
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for infrastructure and industrial projects, necessitating greater adherence to environmental safeguards.

Environmental protection
Conservation
Pollution control
Sustainable development

articles.imageDescription: Illustration of scales balancing industrial development with environmental protection, with a gavel symbolizing the Supreme Court.
articles.trivia: The first major environmental legislation in India was the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Citing Persistent Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• This decision comes amidst persistent retail inflation, which remains above the central bank's comfort zone, driven primarily by food price volatility.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to bring inflation within the target range of 2-6%.
• The MPC also maintained its projection for real GDP growth for FY25, indicating a cautious yet optimistic outlook on economic activity.
• Experts suggest that future rate cuts are contingent on a sustained fall in inflation and global economic stability.

articles.headline: India, France Deepen Strategic Cooperation with Focus on Defence, Space, and Clean Energy
articles.explainer:
• India and France held their annual strategic dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership forged in 1998.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defence industrial cooperation, including joint development and co-production of advanced military platforms.
• Both nations pledged to expand collaboration in space technology, particularly in satellite navigation and Earth observation missions.
• A new roadmap for cooperation in clean energy, including nuclear and renewable sources, was unveiled, aiming to achieve climate targets.
• The leaders also exchanged views on regional and global security challenges, emphasizing a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Bharat AI' Initiative to Foster Indigenous AI Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The Union government today unveiled the 'Bharat AI' initiative, a comprehensive program aimed at boosting indigenous capabilities in Artificial Intelligence.
• The initiative focuses on three key pillars: developing a robust AI computing infrastructure, promoting AI research and innovation, and fostering AI skill development across the workforce.
• It includes provisions for grants to startups working on AI solutions for public good, particularly in healthcare, agriculture, and governance.
• A national AI portal will serve as a hub for data sharing, open-source AI models, and collaboration between academia, industry, and government.
• The move is seen as a strategic step towards achieving self-reliance in cutting-edge technologies and positioning India as a global AI leader.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Alarming Decline in Himalayan Glaciers, Calls for Urgent Climate Action
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM) reveals an accelerated rate of melting of Himalayan glaciers over the last two decades.
• The study, based on satellite imagery and ground observations, indicates a significant reduction in glacier mass balance, threatening water security for millions.
• The report attributes the rapid decline primarily to rising global temperatures and increased black carbon deposition.
• It warns of severe downstream impacts, including increased glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs) in the short term and water scarcity in the long term for major river systems.
• Scientists and policymakers are urged to implement robust climate adaptation and mitigation strategies, focusing on regional cooperation.

articles.headline: BSF Enhances Surveillance Capabilities Along Western Borders with Advanced Drone Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) has significantly upgraded its surveillance infrastructure along India's western borders, particularly with Pakistan, by deploying advanced drone systems.
• These new drones are equipped with high-resolution thermal imaging cameras, night vision capabilities, and AI-powered analytics for real-time threat detection.
• The move aims to counter cross-border infiltration, smuggling of arms and narcotics, and improve situational awareness in challenging terrains.
• BSF personnel are undergoing specialized training to operate and maintain these sophisticated unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs).
• This enhancement is part of a broader strategy to modernize border management and integrate technology for effective security operations.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Advanced Propulsion System for Future Deep Space Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful ground testing of an advanced high-thrust cryogenic engine.
• This new propulsion system is designed to power India's next generation of heavy-lift launch vehicles, crucial for future deep space missions like planetary explorations and human spaceflight.
• The test demonstrated enhanced efficiency, reliability, and extended operational duration compared to existing cryogenic engines.
• Scientists highlighted the indigenous development of this complex technology, marking a significant step towards self-reliance in space exploration.
• This achievement is vital for missions like the proposed Mars Sample Return and Venus orbiter, giving India greater capabilities in the global space race.

articles.headline: NEP 2020: Focus on Vocational Training Integration in Higher Education
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a national conclave to review the progress of integrating vocational training into mainstream higher education as per the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Key discussions revolved around designing credit-based vocational courses, establishing industry partnerships, and ensuring seamless mobility between vocational and general education streams.
• The policy aims to equip students with practical skills, enhance employability, and reduce the skill gap in various industries.
• Universities and colleges are being encouraged to introduce skill-based curricula and offer multiple exit/entry options, making education more flexible and learner-centric.
• Challenges related to faculty training, infrastructure development, and industry alignment were also addressed, with plans for expedited implementation.

articles.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Shows Robust Growth, Indicating Strong Festive Demand
articles.explainer:
• India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) surged to 58.2 in September, up from 56.4 in August, signaling robust expansion in the sector.
• This marks one of the strongest improvements in operating conditions in over a year, driven by a sharp rise in new orders and production.
• The increase is attributed to strong domestic demand, particularly in anticipation of the upcoming festive season, and a modest recovery in export orders.
• Input cost inflation remained elevated, but firms managed to pass on some of these costs, leading to a slight increase in output prices.
• The positive PMI data suggests resilience in the Indian economy and a continued recovery trajectory, despite global headwinds.

articles.trivia: PMI surveys are conducted monthly by S&P Global and are closely watched by economists and investors as a leading indicator of economic health.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Sweeping Reforms in Prison Administration for Human Rights Compliance
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Home Affairs submitted its report recommending comprehensive reforms in prison administration across India.
• The report highlighted issues such as overcrowding, inadequate staff, poor sanitation, and lack of mental health facilities in prisons.
• Key recommendations include the implementation of the 'Model Prisons Act, 2023', increased budget allocation for prison infrastructure, and regular training for prison staff.
• It also emphasized the need for speedy trials, greater use of alternative dispute resolution mechanisms, and de-criminalization of petty offenses to reduce the prison population.
• The committee stressed the importance of treating prisoners with dignity and ensuring their fundamental rights, aligning with international human rights standards.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Global Consensus on Cryptocurrency Regulation at G20 Finance Ministers Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India, at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting, strongly pushed for a globally coordinated approach to regulate cryptocurrencies.
• Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman highlighted the risks posed by unregulated crypto assets, including money laundering, terror financing, and financial instability.
• India emphasized the need for a common framework that considers the diverse economic and technological landscapes of G20 nations.
• The discussions built upon the IMF-FSB synthesis paper on crypto assets, which provides a comprehensive policy and regulatory roadmap.
• A global consensus is deemed crucial to prevent regulatory arbitrage and ensure the stability of the international financial system.

articles.headline: Project Cheetah: New Cohort of Cheetahs Introduced in Kuno National Park, Focus on Genetic Diversity
articles.explainer:
• Under Project Cheetah, a new group of cheetahs, sourced from South Africa, was released into the acclimatization enclosures at Kuno National Park (KNP) in Madhya Pradesh.
• This new cohort aims to bolster the existing population and enhance genetic diversity, which is crucial for the long-term viability of the reintroduction program.
• The previous batches of cheetahs have shown mixed results, with some mortalities, necessitating continuous monitoring and adaptive management strategies.
• Experts emphasize the importance of managing prey base, habitat conditions, and preventing human-wildlife conflict for the success of the project.
• Project Cheetah, launched in 2022, is India's ambitious effort to re-establish the species that went extinct in the country in 1952.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity

articles.trivia: The Asiatic cheetah, native to India, was declared extinct in 1952. Project Cheetah involves the reintroduction of African cheetahs.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Affordable Clean Energy Storage Technology by Indian Researchers
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bengaluru, have announced a significant breakthrough in developing a new, cost-effective energy storage technology.
• The innovation involves a novel solid-state battery design that utilizes abundant and non-toxic materials, making it a sustainable alternative to conventional lithium-ion batteries.
• This technology promises higher energy density, faster charging cycles, and enhanced safety features, crucial for grid-scale energy storage and electric vehicles.
• The indigenous development aims to reduce India's dependence on imported battery components and accelerate the transition to renewable energy sources.
• This development is expected to boost India's clean energy goals and strengthen its position in the global green technology market.

articles.headline: Government Expands 'Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission' to Cover More Remote Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry announced the expansion of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to bring more remote and underserved regions under its ambit.
• The initiative aims to create a seamless online platform for healthcare services, enabling digital health records, teleconsultations, and paperless medical transactions.
• Special focus will be given to improving digital literacy and infrastructure in rural and tribal areas to ensure equitable access.
• The expansion includes setting up more 'Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts' (ABHA) generation camps and training healthcare workers in digital tools.
• ABDM is crucial for enhancing healthcare accessibility, efficiency, and transparency, aligning with the vision of Universal Health Coverage.

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission for Jammu & Kashmir Completes Mandate, Submits Report
articles.explainer:
• The Delimitation Commission for Jammu & Kashmir formally submitted its final report, completing its mandate of redrawing assembly and parliamentary constituencies.
• The commission, constituted after the abrogation of Article 370 and reorganization of J&K into a Union Territory, aimed to ensure equitable representation.
• The report proposes changes in the number of seats for the legislative assembly, with specific reservations for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.
• It also outlines the boundaries of new parliamentary constituencies, taking into account geographical contiguity and population density.
• The completion of delimitation is a crucial step towards holding assembly elections in Jammu & Kashmir, a key aspect of restoring democratic processes.

articles.trivia: Delimitation is typically carried out every ten years after a census, but has been frozen until 2026 to avoid political instability due to population changes.

articles.headline: Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban 2.0: Focus on Circular Economy for Waste Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs reviewed the progress of Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban 2.0, emphasizing the integration of circular economy principles in waste management.
• The mission aims to make all Indian cities 'Garbage Free' by focusing on source segregation, scientific processing of waste, and recovery of resources.
• New guidelines encourage urban local bodies to adopt advanced technologies for waste-to-wealth conversion and promote recycling and reuse.
• Funding mechanisms are being streamlined to support innovative projects in waste management and build robust infrastructure.
• This phase of SBM-U seeks to move beyond basic sanitation to comprehensive and sustainable urban waste management solutions.

• Both nations emphasized the importance of collective action on climate change, sustainable development, and reforms of multilateral institutions.

• The strategic partnership between India and Brazil, both leading emerging economies, is crucial for shaping a more equitable global order.

articles.headline: E-Waste Management Rules, 2024: Emphasis on Extended Producer Responsibility and Circularity
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change notified new E-Waste Management Rules, 2024, replacing previous regulations.
• The updated rules place greater emphasis on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR), mandating producers to collect and recycle a higher percentage of their electronic waste.
• They introduce a system of tradable EPR certificates to facilitate compliance and promote a circular economy for electronic goods.
• The scope of e-waste covered has been expanded to include more categories of electronic and electrical equipment.
• The rules aim to streamline the collection, dismantling, and recycling of e-waste, mitigate environmental pollution, and recover valuable materials.

articles.headline: India's Food Grain Production Projected for Record Kharif Output, Boosting Food Security
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare released its first advance estimates for Kharif crops, projecting a record food grain production for 2024-25.
• Favorable monsoon patterns and timely government support schemes are cited as key reasons for the optimistic outlook.
• Rice, maize, and pulses are expected to see significant increases in yield, contributing to national food security and potentially stabilizing food inflation.
• The government highlighted initiatives like improved seed distribution, irrigation facilities, and minimum support price (MSP) mechanisms as crucial for this success.
• This robust output is expected to strengthen India's agricultural sector and ensure adequate buffer stocks.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in India, consider the following statements:
1. The 'Polluter Pays Principle' is a fundamental aspect often emphasized in EIA judgments.
2. Public consultation is a mandatory component for all projects requiring an Environmental Clearance.
3. The EIA Notification 2020 introduced more lenient regulations for certain industrial projects.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Supreme Court often emphasizes the 'Polluter Pays Principle' in environmental judgments, including those related to EIA. Statement 2 is incorrect: Public consultation is mandatory only for specific categories of projects (A and B1) and not for all projects requiring environmental clearance, especially not for category B2 projects. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Supreme Court ruling upheld amendments to the EIA Notification 2020, which introduced *stricter* regulations, not more lenient ones.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is NOT a primary function of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in its monetary policy operations?
(a) Control of credit
(b) Issuance of currency
(c) Management of foreign exchange
(d) Fixing the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for agricultural products
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) Fixing the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for agricultural products
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary functions of RBI include control of credit, issuance of currency, and management of foreign exchange. Fixing the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for agricultural products is a function of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, based on recommendations from the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP), which falls under fiscal policy, not monetary policy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following pairs regarding India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI):
1. Aadhaar: Digital identity platform
2. UPI: Digital payments system
3. DigiLocker: Digital document wallet
Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three pairs are correctly matched. Aadhaar provides a unique digital identity, UPI facilitates instant digital payments, and DigiLocker serves as a digital wallet for official documents, all forming key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure.

prelimsMocks.question:

(c) Botswana and Zimbabwe
(d) Ethiopia and Sudan
prelimsMocks.explanation: The initial batches of cheetahs for Project Cheetah were sourced from Namibia and South Africa, as these countries have healthy cheetah populations suitable for reintroduction.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is a 'State Subject' under the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution?
(a) Defence
(b) Railways
(c) Prisons
(d) Banking
prelimsMocks.explanation: Prisons are a 'State Subject' under the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution, meaning state governments have the primary responsibility for their administration. Defence, Railways, and Banking are Union Subjects.

GS1.headline: New Report Highlights Alarming Decline in Himalayan Glaciers, Calls for Urgent Climate Action
GS1.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM) reveals an accelerated rate of melting of Himalayan glaciers over the last two decades.
• The study, based on satellite imagery and ground observations, indicates a significant reduction in glacier mass balance, threatening water security for millions.
• The report attributes the rapid decline primarily to rising global temperatures and increased black carbon deposition.
• It warns of severe downstream impacts, including increased glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs) in the short term and water scarcity in the long term for major river systems.
• Scientists and policymakers are urged to implement robust climate adaptation and mitigation strategies, focusing on regional cooperation.

GS2.headline: NEP 2020: Focus on Vocational Training Integration in Higher Education
GS2.explainer:

• Key discussions revolved around designing credit-based vocational courses, establishing industry partnerships, and ensuring seamless mobility between vocational and general education streams.
• The policy aims to equip students with practical skills, enhance employability, and reduce the skill gap in various industries.
• Universities and colleges are being encouraged to introduce skill-based curricula and offer multiple exit/entry options, making education more flexible and learner-centric.
• Challenges related to faculty training, infrastructure development, and industry alignment were also addressed, with plans for expedited implementation.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Sweeping Reforms in Prison Administration for Human Rights Compliance
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Home Affairs submitted its report recommending comprehensive reforms in prison administration across India.
• The report highlighted issues such as overcrowding, inadequate staff, poor sanitation, and lack of mental health facilities in prisons.
• Key recommendations include the implementation of the 'Model Prisons Act, 2023', increased budget allocation for prison infrastructure, and regular training for prison staff.
• It also emphasized the need for speedy trials, greater use of alternative dispute resolution mechanisms, and de-criminalization of petty offenses to reduce the prison population.
• The committee stressed the importance of treating prisoners with dignity and ensuring their fundamental rights, aligning with international human rights standards.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Global Consensus on Cryptocurrency Regulation at G20 Finance Ministers Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India, at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting, strongly pushed for a globally coordinated approach to regulate cryptocurrencies.
• Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman highlighted the risks posed by unregulated crypto assets, including money laundering, terror financing, and financial instability.
• India emphasized the need for a common framework that considers the diverse economic and technological landscapes of G20 nations.
• The discussions built upon the IMF-FSB synthesis paper on crypto assets, which provides a comprehensive policy and regulatory roadmap.
• A global consensus is deemed crucial to prevent regulatory arbitrage and ensure the stability of the international financial system.

GS2.headline: Delimitation Commission for Jammu & Kashmir Completes Mandate, Submits Report
GS2.explainer:
• The Delimitation Commission for Jammu & Kashmir formally submitted its final report, completing its mandate of redrawing assembly and parliamentary constituencies.
• The commission, constituted after the abrogation of Article 370 and reorganization of J&K into a Union Territory, aimed to ensure equitable representation.
• The report proposes changes in the number of seats for the legislative assembly, with specific reservations for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.
• It also outlines the boundaries of new parliamentary constituencies, taking into account geographical contiguity and population density.
• The completion of delimitation is a crucial step towards holding assembly elections in Jammu & Kashmir, a key aspect of restoring democratic processes.

GS3.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Stricter EIA Norms, Prioritizes Environmental Protection
GS3.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, upheld the validity of recent amendments to the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) notification, 2020, which introduced more stringent regulations.
• The ruling emphasizes the 'polluter pays' principle and the need for comprehensive pre-project environmental assessments.
• It rejected arguments for expedited clearances at the cost of ecological integrity, stressing the intergenerational equity aspect of environmental resources.
• The judgment also highlighted the importance of public consultation in the EIA process, making it a non-negotiable component for category A and B1 projects.
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for infrastructure and industrial projects, necessitating greater adherence to environmental safeguards.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Citing Persistent Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• This decision comes amidst persistent retail inflation, which remains above the central bank's comfort zone, driven primarily by food price volatility.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to bring inflation within the target range of 2-6%.
• The MPC also maintained its projection for real GDP growth for FY25, indicating a cautious yet optimistic outlook on economic activity.
• Experts suggest that future rate cuts are contingent on a sustained fall in inflation and global economic stability.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'Bharat AI' Initiative to Foster Indigenous AI Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• The Union government today unveiled the 'Bharat AI' initiative, a comprehensive program aimed at boosting indigenous capabilities in Artificial Intelligence.
• The initiative focuses on three key pillars: developing a robust AI computing infrastructure, promoting AI research and innovation, and fostering AI skill development across the workforce.
• It includes provisions for grants to startups working on AI solutions for public good, particularly in healthcare, agriculture, and governance.
• A national AI portal will serve as a hub for data sharing, open-source AI models, and collaboration between academia, industry, and government.
• The move is seen as a strategic step towards achieving self-reliance in cutting-edge technologies and positioning India as a global AI leader.

GS3.headline: BSF Enhances Surveillance Capabilities Along Western Borders with Advanced Drone Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) has significantly upgraded its surveillance infrastructure along India's western borders, particularly with Pakistan, by deploying advanced drone systems.
• These new drones are equipped with high-resolution thermal imaging cameras, night vision capabilities, and AI-powered analytics for real-time threat detection.
• The move aims to counter cross-border infiltration, smuggling of arms and narcotics, and improve situational awareness in challenging terrains.
• BSF personnel are undergoing specialized training to operate and maintain these sophisticated unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs).
• This enhancement is part of a broader strategy to modernize border management and integrate technology for effective security operations.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Advanced Propulsion System for Future Deep Space Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful ground testing of an advanced high-thrust cryogenic engine.
• This new propulsion system is designed to power India's next generation of heavy-lift launch vehicles, crucial for future deep space missions like planetary explorations and human spaceflight.
• The test demonstrated enhanced efficiency, reliability, and extended operational duration compared to existing cryogenic engines.
• Scientists highlighted the indigenous development of this complex technology, marking a significant step towards self-reliance in space exploration.
• This achievement is vital for missions like the proposed Mars Sample Return and Venus orbiter, giving India greater capabilities in the global space race.

GS3.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Shows Robust Growth, Indicating Strong Festive Demand
GS3.explainer:
• India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) surged to 58.2 in September, up from 56.4 in August, signaling robust expansion in the sector.
• This marks one of the strongest improvements in operating conditions in over a year, driven by a sharp rise in new orders and production.
• The increase is attributed to strong domestic demand, particularly in anticipation of the upcoming festive season, and a modest recovery in export orders.
• Input cost inflation remained elevated, but firms managed to pass on some of these costs, leading to a slight increase in output prices.
• The positive PMI data suggests resilience in the Indian economy and a continued recovery trajectory, despite global headwinds.

GS3.headline: Project Cheetah: New Cohort of Cheetahs Introduced in Kuno National Park, Focus on Genetic Diversity
GS3.explainer:
• Under Project Cheetah, a new group of cheetahs, sourced from South Africa, was released into the acclimatization enclosures at Kuno National Park (KNP) in Madhya Pradesh.
• This new cohort aims to bolster the existing population and enhance genetic diversity, which is crucial for the long-term viability of the reintroduction program.
• The previous batches of cheetahs have shown mixed results, with some mortalities, necessitating continuous monitoring and adaptive management strategies.
• Experts emphasize the importance of managing prey base, habitat conditions, and preventing human-wildlife conflict for the success of the project.
• Project Cheetah, launched in 2022, is India's ambitious effort to re-establish the species that went extinct in the country in 1952.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Affordable Clean Energy Storage Technology by Indian Researchers
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bengaluru, have announced a significant breakthrough in developing a new, cost-effective energy storage technology.
• The innovation involves a novel solid-state battery design that utilizes abundant and non-toxic materials, making it a sustainable alternative to conventional lithium-ion batteries.
• This technology promises higher energy density, faster charging cycles, and enhanced safety features, crucial for grid-scale energy storage and electric vehicles.
• The indigenous development aims to reduce India's dependence on imported battery components and accelerate the transition to renewable energy sources.
• This development is expected to boost India's clean energy goals and strengthen its position in the global green technology market.

GS3.headline: Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban 2.0: Focus on Circular Economy for Waste Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs reviewed the progress of Swachh Bharat Mission-Urban 2.0, emphasizing the integration of circular economy principles in waste management.
• The mission aims to make all Indian cities 'Garbage Free' by focusing on source segregation, scientific processing of waste, and recovery of resources.
• New guidelines encourage urban local bodies to adopt advanced technologies for waste-to-wealth conversion and promote recycling and reuse.
• Funding mechanisms are being streamlined to support innovative projects in waste management and build robust infrastructure.
• This phase of SBM-U seeks to move beyond basic sanitation to comprehensive and sustainable urban waste management solutions.

GS3.headline: E-Waste Management Rules, 2024: Emphasis on Extended Producer Responsibility and Circularity
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change notified new E-Waste Management Rules, 2024, replacing previous regulations.
• The updated rules place greater emphasis on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR), mandating producers to collect and recycle a higher percentage of their electronic waste.
• They introduce a system of tradable EPR certificates to facilitate compliance and promote a circular economy for electronic goods.
• The scope of e-waste covered has been expanded to include more categories of electronic and electrical equipment.
• The rules aim to streamline the collection, dismantling, and recycling of e-waste, mitigate environmental pollution, and recover valuable materials.

GS3.headline: India's Food Grain Production Projected for Record Kharif Output, Boosting Food Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare released its first advance estimates for Kharif crops, projecting a record food grain production for 2024-25.
• Favorable monsoon patterns and timely government support schemes are cited as key reasons for the optimistic outlook.
• Rice, maize, and pulses are expected to see significant increases in yield, contributing to national food security and potentially stabilizing food inflation.
• The government highlighted initiatives like improved seed distribution, irrigation facilities, and minimum support price (MSP) mechanisms as crucial for this success.
• This robust output is expected to strengthen India's agricultural sector and ensure adequate buffer stocks.
